findings.message: This file dates PR #148's merge as 2026-06-04, but the bundle README and evaluation.md both place the same merge on 2026-06-02. The evidence set is internally inconsistent about the snapshot boundary.
The bullet says `"tmp/reviewer-runtime"` is defined in four separate locations, but the rationale says five occurrences and the locations list contains five paths. The count in the heading is wrong.

`evaluation.md` says the overall density drop is "fully concentrated in cli and cli-composition", but the table immediately above shows domain density also fell from 0.743 to 0.713 (-4.0%). The statement should say the drop is mostly, not fully, concentrated in those two crates.

`evaluation.md` says the before/after census used the same model `claude-sonnet-4-6`, but the checked-in workflow documents and implements a mixed model setup: Sonnet for scan/load/synthesis and Opus for adversarial verification (`dry-scan.workflow.js` lines 6-7 and 335-346). This makes the methodology claim internally inconsistent; update the fairness-control wording to describe the model mix, or adjust the workflow/provenance if Sonnet-only was intended.

The report generator labels every cross-layer finding as “Cross-layer (knowledge) duplications — the DRY gate's prime targets”, but it only filters on cross-layer locations and includes exact-clone/near-clone/data-dup entries, while the evaluation says the gate targets semantic diff-vs-corpus duplication and structurally misses cross-layer knowledge/data duplication. This makes the generated before/after reports contradict the bundle’s own gate model and misclassify the highlighted section.

T011/T012 are not executable as written: both tasks place the shared constant in `libs/infrastructure` as `pub(crate)`, but the listed consumers include `apps/cli` and `apps/cli-composition`, which are different crates and cannot import a `pub(crate)` item from infrastructure. This needs either a public re-export/shared module or a different home for the constants.

AC-03 requires lock acquisition and 4-source git-diff union to be extracted to a shared module, but the implementation plan explicitly defers git-diff union and T006/T007 only cover subprocess helpers plus SHA-256. `task-coverage.json` still marks AC-03 as covered by T006/T007, so completing the plan can leave AC-03 unsatisfied. Update the spec/acceptance criteria or add tasks so the accepted D3 deferral is represented consistently.

IN-04 includes consolidating track-directory magic strings and other constants, but the D4 implementation tasks only cover CwdGuard, git init helpers, stub bindings, POLL_INTERVAL, and REVIEWER_RUNTIME_DIR. `task-coverage.json` marks IN-04 fully covered by T008-T012 even though none of those tasks addresses the track-directory string consolidation called out by the spec, leaving in-scope work without an executable task.

AC-03 requires the D3 remediation to extract the Codex subprocess timeout-poll loop, but T006 only schedules `spawn_codex`, `drain_pipe`, `tee_stderr_to_file`, and the runtime-path builder, while T007 only handles SHA-256 hex delegation. As written, the implementation plan can complete all listed D3 tasks while leaving the timeout-poll loop outside any task, so the claimed AC-03 coverage is incomplete.

T008 directs the shared `CwdGuard`/`CurrentDirGuard` definition into a `#[cfg(test)]` location and then has separate crates (`apps/cli`, `apps/cli-composition`) import it. In Rust, `cfg(test)` items in the infrastructure crate are not exported when that crate is compiled as a dependency of another crate's tests, so this task is not executable as described; it needs a dev-visible public test-support API, feature, or separate test-support crate.

`impl-plan.json` still tells T008/T009 to create a dev-visible shared test-support API that dependent crates import, including a workspace dev-dependency/public feature and a public API. That contradicts ADR D4 and spec IN-04/AC-04, which deliberately scope these helpers to `#[cfg(test)]` common test-support modules and exclude the earlier cross-crate dev-visible API approach. An implementer following these tasks would add an out-of-scope API instead of the reconciled design, so the D4 tasks/rendered plan need to be realigned with the ADR/spec before implementation.

T009 requires one `#[cfg(test)]` apps/cli helper to replace both `apps/cli/tests/transition_integration.rs` and the unit-test helpers under `apps/cli/src/...`. Those are different Cargo test crates: integration tests cannot use the binary crate's `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate)` module, while `tests/common` is not normally visible to source unit tests. As written, the task is infeasible without either keeping duplicate helpers, moving tests, or introducing a dev-visible helper that the plan explicitly forbids, so AC-04 cannot be satisfied by the described implementation.

T011, and the same instruction in T012, makes the plan infeasible: it tells implementers to promote `apps/cli` to a production dependency on `libs/infrastructure` while also requiring `cargo make ci` to pass. The repository architecture rules allow the `cli` crate to depend only on `cli_composition`, so this implementation path will fail the layer check; the shared constant needs to be consumed without adding a direct CLI -> infrastructure production dependency.

T012 lists existing `"tmp/reviewer-runtime"` inline literals, then excludes them as "call-site literals" and only requires replacing the three const definitions. That contradicts IN-04/AC-05, which require this value to be consolidated to a single const per architectural-layer boundary; the current task can leave same-boundary runtime-dir literals outside remediation while still claiming D4 is complete.

AC-05 cites ADR D4 but changes the constants remediation from D4's single `const` definitions into one const per architecture boundary, and explicitly treats cross-boundary occurrences as non-violations. That lets T011/T012/T013 complete while still leaving duplicate `POLL_INTERVAL` or `tmp/reviewer-runtime` values across cli/infrastructure boundaries, so the plan no longer satisfies the D4 data-dup cleanup it claims to implement.

`related_conventions` makes `hexagonal-architecture.md#Layer Dependencies` required reading, but that section says the CLI may depend on `infrastructure`, while AC-05/T011/T012 rely on `architecture-rules.json` forbidding `cli -> infrastructure` and explicitly prohibit introducing that dependency. This leaves the constants tasks with contradictory layer guidance: following the cited convention would produce the dependency the acceptance criteria says must not exist.
